title: The President Wore Pearls

jokes.text: Finally, they'll teach our kids the dangers of doubling down on a six.
jokes.setup: Casino night at elementary school presented as educational
jokes.punchline: Homer genuinely thinks it will teach responsible gambling by example
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - missing the obvious irony while sounding reasonable. Well-integrated character moment.

jokes.text: I really shouldn't be here. I have a problem with games of chance.
jokes.setup: Marge expressing concern about gambling addiction
jokes.punchline: Sets up the escalating gambling problem revelation
jokes.explanation: Good character setup that pays off with the Candy Land revelation. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I played Candy Land with Maggie and ended up throwing vodka in her face.
jokes.punchline: Shockingly inappropriate escalation from children's board game to baby abuse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from innocent game to shocking behavior. Perfect economy and memorable absurdity.

jokes.text: Bet? System? Betting system? God is telling me to gamble!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to reassure Marge about her gambling
jokes.punchline: Marge's word association spiraling into divine gambling mandate
jokes.explanation: Great escalating word association leading to absurd religious justification for gambling.

jokes.text: My fee for this evening goes to victims of my criminal recidivism.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning his ongoing criminal activities in formal language
jokes.explanation: Good character moment mixing formal vocabulary with casual crime admission.

jokes.text: So relax, have fun and please God don't piss me off.
jokes.punchline: Polite host demeanor suddenly dropping to threatening mobster
jokes.explanation: Classic Fat Tony character beat - polite veneer dropping to reveal threat.

jokes.text: I got the idea from an episode of Saved by the Bell. It was always on too late for me.
jokes.punchline: Nerdy kid's bedtime preventing him from watching teen show
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Martin's sheltered, regimented life.

jokes.text: Woo-hoo! D'oh! D'oh! D'oh...
jokes.punchline: Rapid alternation between winning and losing reactions
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer catchphrase usage. Functional visual gag but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: First thing tomorrow, we're getting a PlayStation One.
jokes.punchline: Outdated gaming system as ultimate prize
jokes.explanation: Mild joke about outdated technology as dream purchase. Adequate but forgettable.

jokes.text: Give me most of it in cash and the rest in suitcases to carry the cash.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to cash in chips
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous logistics planning for massive payout
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation showing Bart's grandiose thinking. Good economy and absurd logic.

jokes.text: I'll be bringin' sparkplugs to chuck at people I don't like, like you!
jokes.setup: Willie describing day at ballpark prize
jokes.punchline: Immediately targeting the person he's talking to
jokes.explanation: Good Willie character moment - hostile and specific threat delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: Think I won't manhandle a little boy?
jokes.punchline: Mobster casually threatening child violence
jokes.explanation: Typical Fat Tony threat but the child target makes it more absurd than threatening.

jokes.text: Look at me! I'm a big man! But I break just like a little girl.
jokes.setup: Milhouse acting tough after apparent victory
jokes.punchline: Immediately deflating with honest self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse character moment - brief confidence immediately undercut by realistic self-knowledge.

jokes.text: I should've known. The same thing happened on Saved by the Bell.
jokes.punchline: Martin referencing his earlier inspiration source
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing Martin should have learned from his source material.

jokes.text: I'll have that milk now. Easy there. That's whole milk. I'm a private citizen now! I can drink what I like!
jokes.punchline: Treating whole milk as forbidden presidential luxury
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take on post-office freedom. Perfectly captures bureaucratic mentality.

jokes.text: You won't have Martin Prince to kick around anymore!
jokes.punchline: Nixon resignation quote applied to elementary school politics
jokes.explanation: Good historical reference applied to trivial situation. Works for Martin's character.

jokes.text: We're from Acme Amusement Rental. We're looking for Principal Skinner. Uh. Yes, we all are. Whoo!
jokes.punchline: Skinner's panicked attempt to hide/blend in
jokes.explanation: Standard evasion gag. Skinner's cowardly character but nothing exceptional.

jokes.text: I'm not saying I have all the answers, but I do have all the answer keys to every test.
jokes.punchline: Replacing policy promises with academic cheating
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson character moment - practical corruption over idealism. Great misdirection structure.

jokes.text: Fractions, dinosaurs, foreign money, the first Thanksgiving.
jokes.setup: Nelson listing what he has answer keys for
jokes.punchline: Random elementary school subjects treated as criminal enterprises
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the answer key joke. Specific subjects add authenticity.

jokes.text: Real estate license exam. My ticket to freedom!
jokes.punchline: Bully seeing real estate as liberation path
jokes.explanation: Great character detail showing Nelson's unexpected ambitions beyond bullying.

jokes.text: What a delicious quiche! I drive a pink Miata!
jokes.setup: Bart trying to spread rumors about Nelson
jokes.punchline: Stereotypical 'effeminate' statements in falsetto
jokes.explanation: Lazy stereotyping humor. Relies on outdated gender assumptions rather than clever writing.

jokes.text: Honey, you could be popular. You've just gotta be yourself in a whole new way!
jokes.punchline: Contradictory advice about authenticity
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about contradictory self-help advice. Good economy and irony.

jokes.text: My name is Nelson. I use a salad fork. La-dee-dah! I wash my face!
jokes.punchline: Basic hygiene as effeminate behavior
jokes.explanation: Slightly better than previous attempt - face washing as sophistication is more absurd.

jokes.text: Where did you get that tutu? Clothesline.
jokes.punchline: Casual theft admission
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery. Bart's casual criminality delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: What is she yakkin' about? I rest my case.
jokes.setup: Lisa speaking French to demonstrate need for French teacher
jokes.punchline: Student's confusion proving Lisa's point
jokes.explanation: Nice demonstration of the problem through audience reaction. Good economy.

jokes.text: Lisa's campaign song about being a bookworm
jokes.punchline: Lisa embracing her unpopular studious image in song
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's authenticity vs. political necessity. Song format reduces economy.

jokes.text: Hey, I've got a song, too. I am Iron Man! Vote for me! Ah, screw it.
jokes.setup: Nelson responding to Lisa's elaborate campaign song
jokes.punchline: Brief heavy metal reference then giving up immediately
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson character moment - minimal effort followed by immediate abandonment. Great economy and misdirection.

jokes.text: Wow, she even beat perennial write-in candidate 'Skinner Sucks.'
jokes.punchline: Skinner acknowledging regular votes against him
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about Skinner's unpopularity. Efficient delivery.

jokes.text: She's captured their imagination like a bright piece of construction paper.
jokes.punchline: Profound comparison deflating to mundane classroom supply
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection from inspirational metaphor to banal reality. Perfect adult-to-child perspective shift.

jokes.text: Well, if you ask me, a woman's weakness is her sense of vanity.
jokes.punchline: Woman suggesting sexist stereotype as strategy
jokes.explanation: Moderate irony in female character promoting gender stereotype. Character consistent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: So when's my inauguration? Hmm. Let's see. When hell freezes over, tough guy.
jokes.punchline: Skinner's hostile dismissal of eight-year-old
jokes.explanation: Good Skinner petty hostility moment. Calling a child 'tough guy' adds absurdity.

jokes.text: Lisa's makeover montage song
jokes.setup: Adult scheme to make Lisa more presentable
jokes.punchline: Elaborate transformation sequence for elementary student
jokes.explanation: Standard makeover parody. Musical format reduces joke density and economy.

jokes.text: Like you're the wife of a businessman. I wish I'd married a businessman. Then I'd have nice things.
jokes.punchline: Marge's personal dissatisfaction emerging
jokes.explanation: Good Marge character moment - compliment turning into personal regret. Relatable adult dissatisfaction.

jokes.text: Is our Frisbee now.
jokes.setup: Lisa mentioning taking playground back from gypsies
jokes.punchline: Stereotypical gypsy claiming ownership of random object
jokes.explanation: Lazy ethnic stereotype humor. Relies on outdated prejudice rather than clever writing.

jokes.text: Look at me. I'm Milhouse. I tuck my shirt into me underpants. I've got no friends. So I confide in Willie.
jokes.punchline: Accurate but cruel impression of lonely Milhouse
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe humor showing adult cruelty. Willie's accent adds character specificity.

jokes.text: Oh, hello, Lisa. We were just discussing Vasco da Gama.
jokes.punchline: Obviously false cover story
jokes.explanation: Standard caught-in-the-act excuse. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: And I'll have hypnotherapy when?
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting early morning student meetings
jokes.punchline: Teacher's sarcastic priority concern
jokes.explanation: Good teacher selfishness moment. Hypnotherapy as priority over students is appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: I've got a question. You're crazy.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking about student phone access to teachers
jokes.punchline: Blunt dismissal disguised as engagement
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and economy. Teacher hostility delivered as false engagement.

jokes.text: Move the brush fire hazard needle?
jokes.punchline: Meaningless bureaucratic task disguised as responsibility
jokes.explanation: Good bureaucratic absurdity. Specific enough to feel authentic.

jokes.text: It's like you're Harry Potter without the magic and wonder.
jokes.punchline: Cultural reference completely undermined by qualifier
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection structure. Harry Potter comparison immediately deflated removes all appeal.

jokes.text: That little girl should learn to read things before she signs them.
jokes.punchline: Hypocritical advice from person exploiting her trust
jokes.explanation: Standard villain irony. Decent character work but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Boy, she'll believe anything. 'Key release boilerplate'? I find that very hard to believe. Mmm-hmm. And yet there it is.
jokes.punchline: Chalmers pointing out obvious lie, Skinner doubling down
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics showing Skinner's weak manipulation skills and Chalmers' skepticism.

jokes.text: You're like Geraldine Ferraro. Except you won where she failed miserably.
jokes.setup: Homer comparing Lisa to historical figure
jokes.punchline: Compliment immediately undermined by harsh assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - supportive intent undermined by tactless delivery. Great structure.

jokes.text: I was being sarcastic. You were? No, I was being sincere. Oh, I'm so confused.
jokes.punchline: Homer's confusion about sarcasm levels
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about understanding sarcasm. Homer's confusion is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: On my chess set, the pawns are all Hamburglars.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Skinner using Lisa as pawn
jokes.punchline: Bart's random McDonald's character chess set
jokes.explanation: Good Bart non-sequitur. Hamburglar chess pieces is specific and absurd enough to work.

jokes.text: Why don't you just give me a sandwich board that says 'male prostitute'?
jokes.punchline: Extreme overreaction to innocent merchandise
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from t-shirt to prostitution. Chalmers' dignity concerns taken to absurd extreme.

jokes.text: Hello, self-esteem! Repo man! Ahhh!
jokes.punchline: Gym equipment being repossessed mid-achievement
jokes.explanation: Excellent physical comedy timing. Moment of triumph immediately destroyed by bureaucratic efficiency.

jokes.text: I'll be cooking my Lean Cuisine in your kiln tonight!
jokes.punchline: Skinner's petty personal use of confiscated equipment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner petty vindictiveness. Specific detail about Lean Cuisine adds authenticity.

jokes.text: I'll be cooking my Lean Cuisine in your kiln tonight!
jokes.punchline: Skinner's plan to use art equipment for mundane personal cooking
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Skinner's petty opportunism and sad personal life

jokes.text: Lisa is a sellout. Lisa is a sellout.
jokes.punchline: Bart's sing-song taunting
jokes.explanation: Standard sibling taunting. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: They eliminated English for Fat Kids! Okay, you'll do!
jokes.setup: Nelson complaining about band elimination affecting his bullying
jokes.punchline: Immediately targeting available substitute victim
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson character moment showing his opportunistic bullying adaptation. Great economy and character integration.

jokes.text: And it wasn't exactly San Diego State to begin with.
jokes.punchline: Specific mediocre college as comparison point
jokes.explanation: Decent reference joke. San Diego State as epitome of mediocrity is specific enough to work.

jokes.text: There's Bugs Bunny, Fat Elvis, Autumn in New England, Bats of the Southwest, all heroes.
jokes.setup: Homer showing stamps as examples of heroes
jokes.punchline: Random stamp subjects classified as heroic
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic failure. Treating stamp subjects as heroes regardless of category is appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: Do you want to see Elvis kiss a bat? Hey, baby, come here. I want to kiss you. No, don't kiss me. You tricked me into betraying my fellow students!
jokes.setup: Homer offering to show stamp interaction
jokes.punchline: Lisa's emotional breakdown interrupting Homer's stamp theater
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Homer's frivolity and Lisa's genuine distress. Emotional timing works well.

jokes.text: Look at your constitution. It's written on the back of a place mat. And not a good place mat. It's from someplace called 'Doodles.'
jokes.punchline: Escalating dismissal of place mat quality
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from bad place mat to specifically terrible restaurant. 'Doodles' is perfectly dismissive.

jokes.text: Any student caught striking will be severely disciplined unless all of you do it. Then I'm stymied.
jokes.setup: Skinner trying to stop student strike
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing the logical flaw in his disciplinary system
jokes.explanation: Excellent Skinner moment showing his bureaucratic mind accidentally undermining himself.

jokes.text: Our budget is stretched tighter than Mother's sauna pants. Seymour! Quit using me in analogies. Yes, Mother.
jokes.punchline: His mother interrupting to complain about being used in metaphors
jokes.explanation: Perfect character interaction showing Skinner's dominated relationship with his mother even in professional settings.

jokes.text: What about gym? Meh.
jokes.setup: Lisa demanding return of music and art
jokes.punchline: Students' indifference to physical education
jokes.explanation: Good economy with single word reaction. Captures student priorities accurately.

jokes.text: Why didn't I cancel Sign Making and Creative Chanting?
jokes.punchline: Skinner regretting not eliminating the tools of protest
jokes.explanation: Smart self-aware humor about protest tools. Skinner's authoritarian thinking exposed.

jokes.text: Would you be willing to pay an extra $1.23 in taxes to fund them? No way! I'm saving for a speedboat.
jokes.setup: Public asked to fund school programs
jokes.punchline: Refusing tiny amount for frivolous personal purchase
jokes.explanation: Good observation about public spending priorities. Speedboat as luxury vs. education funding.

jokes.text: You do? Cool. A blank check for mayhem.
jokes.setup: Marge saying she expects trouble from Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart interpreting parental expectation as permission
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart logic - turning criticism into authorization. Excellent character integration and economy.

jokes.text: Those Fudruckers!
jokes.setup: Marge wanting to swear about situation
jokes.punchline: Restaurant name as substitute profanity
jokes.explanation: Good Marge character moment using restaurant name as clean profanity substitute.

jokes.text: Remember, boys. These are little kids. So take out your tiny batons.
jokes.setup: Police arriving to handle student strike
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's misguided attempt at proportional force
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum absurdity - having child-sized weapons for beating children is somehow more appropriate.

jokes.text: You mean, the mayor who kept me waiting for two hours in that restaurant? I ate so much bread...
jokes.setup: Lisa asking about mayor not caring about police
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's petty restaurant grievance and bread overconsumption
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum character moment showing his petty concerns and weakness for free bread.

jokes.text: Well, I can't take that rattlesnake out of his mailbox now, now can I? Yes, Chief, you could. Well, let me ask you this. Shut up.
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's bizarre revenge plot and illogical defense
jokes.explanation: Great Wiggum character moment showing his petty vindictiveness and poor logic.

jokes.text: Kids who don't get to take music and art are 10% more likely to become chronically unemployed and appear in one of my movies. Where did you get that statistic? Your mother!
jokes.punchline: Obviously made-up statistic with childish comeback
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity parody showing Moore's documentary style with juvenile response.

jokes.text: Zombie eat brains but zombie cannot swallow this injustice.
jokes.punchline: Mixing zombie speech pattern with labor activism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific humor mixing zombie tropes with union solidarity. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Her brother had this to say. Lisa is a nut. She has a rubber butt. Every time she turns around, it goes putt putt.
jokes.punchline: Bart delivering playground rhyme as analysis
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between serious news format and childish content. Character-appropriate for Bart.

jokes.text: All we wanted were hats with a wee light on top. Then one day the mine collapsed. No one made it out alive. Not even Willie!
jokes.punchline: Tragic ending where narrator also died
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection and dark humor. Willie casually revealing his own death in the story is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I don't mean to interrupt your advice from the janitor, but I say cut off the head and the body will die.
jokes.punchline: Edna's class-conscious dismissal followed by violent metaphor
jokes.explanation: Standard class dismissal humor with moderately clever metaphor. Decent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Oh, this place is paradise! Well, I guess this story has a happy ending after all. Just like my last massage.
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriate massage comparison
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer inappropriate comment. Character-consistent but relies on innuendo.

jokes.text: I'm not driving 45 minutes a day! But this is my dream. Oh, why can't you have a normal dream like being an Olympic figure skater?
jokes.setup: Lisa wanting to attend better school
jokes.punchline: Homer's contradictory parenting about dreams and effort
jokes.explanation: Great Homer logic contradiction - wanting Lisa to have ambitious dreams while avoiding any effort.

jokes.text: Are you crazy? I'm not getting up at 6:30 every morning so you can prance around a frozen pond and think you're better than me. Okay! Over my dead body!
jokes.setup: Lisa agreeing to figure skating suggestion
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately rejecting his own suggestion with escalating hostility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - suggesting something then immediately violently opposing it. Great escalation and character consistency.

tmdb_overview: In this loose parody of the musical/movie Evita, Lisa is elected Student Body president, but is unaware that the school staff is using her for their own devices and as a scapegoat if something goes wrong.
